_meta.description: Scoring provenance: maps each Table 3 cell (5 levels x 5 consolidated dimensions) to its source evidence from the 32 raw sub-dimensions in moodle_scores.json

consolidated_dimensions.COV: Coverage and Classification — breadth and taxonomic quality of infrastructure for this level
consolidated_dimensions.ACC: External Accessibility — whether an external agent can programmatically access this capability
consolidated_dimensions.EDU: Educational Specificity — whether the capability carries pedagogically meaningful information
consolidated_dimensions.TMP: Temporal Availability — whether the capability operates in real time or only batch/retrospective
consolidated_dimensions.INT: Cross-Subsystem Integration — whether this capability connects to adjacent subsystems to enable agent workflows

_meta.scoring_method: Each consolidated cell is derived by mapping one or more raw sub-dimensions to the consolidated dimension, converting raw 0-4 scores to the 0/25/50/75/100 rubric scale, and averaging. Where a raw sub-dimension spans two consolidated dimensions, it contributes to both with the noted weight.

COV.derivation: Single source: 1.1 Event Coverage scored 3 (Substantial) = 75. 228 core event classes with LEVEL_PARTICIPATING/TEACHING/OTHER classification.
COV.evidence: lib/classes/event/ — 228 event classes; base_event.php defines edulevel constants (LEVEL_PARTICIPATING, LEVEL_TEACHING, LEVEL_OTHER); CRUD classification via 'c','r','u','d' constants.

ACC.derivation: Single source: 1.2 Observer Subscription scored 2 (Partial) = 50. Internal observers exist via db/events.php but no external streaming API.
ACC.evidence: db/events.php observer registration; lib/classes/event/manager.php dispatch; no WebSocket/SSE endpoint; external access requires plugin deployment.

EDU.derivation: Single source: 1.3 SRL Phase Observability scored 2 (Partial) = 50. Performance phase strong (quiz attempts, submissions), Forethought phase weak (no goal-setting events).
EDU.evidence: mod/quiz/classes/event/ — 15+ attempt events; mod/assign/classes/event/ — submission events; NO events for goal formulation, study scheduling, or strategy selection. Reflection partially covered via mod/workshop peer assessment events.

TMP.derivation: Single source: 1.4 Real-Time External Access scored 1 (Nascent) = 25. Log store polling only via mdl_logstore_standard_log, no streaming.
TMP.evidence: admin/tool/log/store/ — database log store only; no WebSocket, no Server-Sent Events, no message queue integration; agents must poll mdl_logstore_standard_log table.

INT.derivation: Single source: 1.5 Event Enrichment scored 3 (Substantial) = 75. Context hierarchy (system/course/module) and other[] field present, but no prior-state capture.
INT.evidence: base_event.php: contextlevel, contextinstanceid, courseid, userid, relateduserid, other[] array; context hierarchy enables cross-subsystem joins; gap: no before/after state diff in events.

L1_Event_Sensing.discrepancy_note: DISCREPANCY: Raw 5-dimension average = 55%. Paper Table 3 reports 72%. The 72% figure was scored in a separate pass and is NOT derivable from the 32 raw sub-dimensions. This provenance file corrects the scores to match the documented evidence.

COV.derivation: Average of 2.1 (75) and 2.2 (75) = 75. Rich raw data plus ML analytics pipeline.
COV.evidence: mdl_grade_grades, mdl_course_modules_completion, mdl_competency tables; analytics/classes/ — 8 prediction targets including dropout risk; ML pipeline with Python backend and time-splitting strategies.

ACC.derivation: Single source: 2.4 API Query Capability scored 3 (Substantial) = 75. 402 web service functions with token auth.
ACC.evidence: lib/externallib.php — 402 web service functions; REST and SOAP protocols; token-based authentication; covers grades, completion, enrolment, competencies.

EDU.derivation: Single source: 2.3 Unified Learner Model scored 1 (Nascent) = 25. Data scattered across tables with no unified student profile API.
EDU.evidence: Student state fragmented: mdl_grade_grades, mdl_course_modules_completion, mdl_competency, mdl_analytics_predictions — no unified query endpoint; agent must assemble profile from 4+ independent queries.

TMP.derivation: Single source: 2.5 Temporal Analytics scored 2 (Partial) = 50. Time-splitting strategies exist but no real-time session tracking.
TMP.evidence: analytics/classes/local/time_splitting/ — quarterly, weekly, past-3-days strategies; predictions run on cron schedule, not real-time; no session-level temporal analytics.

INT.derivation: Average of 2.6 (50) and 2.3 integration aspect (25) = 37.5, rounded to 38. Privacy system exists but no agent consent model; learner model fragmentation means cross-subsystem queries require manual joins.
INT.evidence: Role-based access via capabilities system (access/capability.php); no agent-specific permission model; analytics predictions NOT exposed via web services — internal only.

L2_Student_State.discrepancy_note: DISCREPANCY: Consolidated 5-dimension average = 50%. Paper reports 58%. Raw 6-dimension average = 58.3%. The paper's 58% matches the raw system, not the consolidated system.

COV.derivation: Single source: 3.1 AI Subsystem Infrastructure scored 2 (Partial) = 50. 6 providers and 4 action types exist but are user-initiated only.
COV.evidence: ai/classes/provider/ — openai, azureai, awsbedrock, gemini, deepseek, ollama; ai/classes/action/ — generate_text, generate_image, summarise, explain; all require user-initiated placement trigger, no autonomous invocation.

ACC.derivation: Single source: 3.5 Write-Back Capability scored 2 (Partial) = 50. Web services exist for grades and messages but no agent-specific write API.
ACC.evidence: core_message_send_instant_messages, core_grades_update_grades web services exist; no agent-attributed write channel; interventions not distinguishable from instructor actions in the database.

EDU.derivation: Average of 3.2 (0) and 3.3 (0) = 0. No agent role, no reasoning pipeline, no pedagogical reasoning whatsoever.
EDU.evidence: No agent role in mdl_role table or capabilities system; no planner, goal-setter, or reasoning chain in ai/classes/; all AI actions are stateless text/image generation.

TMP.derivation: Average of 3.6 (25) and 3.7 (25) = 25. Dropout prediction exists in analytics but is batch; placements exist but are not learner-context-adaptive.
TMP.evidence: analytics/classes/prediction/ — dropout models run on cron; ai/classes/placement/ — courseassist and editor placements are static, not adapted to learner state or timing.

INT.derivation: Single source: 3.4 AI-Analytics Bridge scored 0 (Absent) = 0. Zero cross-imports between core_ai and core_analytics.
INT.evidence: grep -r 'analytics' ai/classes/ = 0 results; grep -r 'ai/' analytics/classes/ = 0 results; no shared database tables; no event-channel subscriptions; complete code-level and data-level isolation.

COV.derivation: Single source: 4.1 Agent Registry scored 0 (Absent) = 0. No multi-agent registry or discovery mechanism.
COV.evidence: No agent registry table, no agent discovery service, no agent type system in any Moodle subsystem.

ACC.derivation: Single source: 4.2 Shared State Store scored 0 (Absent) = 0. No shared agent memory or coordination state.
ACC.evidence: No shared state table, no coordination cache, no agent-to-agent data channel.

EDU.derivation: Average of 4.3 (0) and 4.4 (0) = 0. No inter-agent communication, no conflict resolution, no pedagogical coordination.
EDU.evidence: No message protocol between agents; no priority system; no mechanism to prevent conflicting simultaneous interventions.

TMP.derivation: Average of 4.3 (0) and 4.4 (0) = 0. No real-time coordination mechanism.
TMP.evidence: Same as EDU — no coordination infrastructure exists at any temporal resolution.

INT.derivation: Single source: 4.5 Scalability Architecture scored 1 (Nascent) = 25. Task system exists but is not agent-aware.
INT.evidence: lib/classes/task/ — scheduled_task and adhoc_task infrastructure; caching via MUC (Moodle Universal Cache); role-based permissions. These are repurposable building blocks, not agent coordination infrastructure.

L4_Orchestration.discrepancy_note: DISCREPANCY: Consolidated average (0+0+0+0+25)/5 = 5%. Paper reports 8%. The 5% matches the raw system.

COV.derivation: Average of 5.1 (50) and 5.3 (50) = 50. Two delivery channels exist: messaging and AI content generation.
COV.evidence: message/lib.php — messaging API; ai/classes/action/generate_text.php, generate_image.php — content generation. Both functional but not connected to intervention pipeline.

ACC.derivation: Single source: 5.2 UI Injection scored 1 (Nascent) = 25. Output renderers and blocks exist but no real-time content injection.
ACC.evidence: lib/outputrenderers.php — rendering framework; blocks/ — block plugin system; no mechanism to inject content into an active learning activity in real time.

EDU.derivation: Average of 5.4 (25) and 5.5 (0) = 12.5, rounded to 13. Grade feedback exists but no intervention tracking, no outcome measurement, no timing intelligence.
EDU.evidence: Grade feedback via mdl_assign_grades.feedback; no intervention effectiveness tracking; no link between AI action and subsequent student behaviour change; no optimal-timing engine.

TMP.derivation: Single source: 5.5 Timing Intelligence scored 0 (Absent) = 0. No real-time delivery, no engagement-aware scheduling.
TMP.evidence: No optimal-timing engine; no engagement-state detection; interventions cannot be triggered by real-time learner state changes.

INT.derivation: Single source: 5.4 Feedback Loop scored 1 (Nascent) = 25. Grade feedback exists but interventions are not tracked or connected to outcomes.
INT.evidence: Feedback stored in mdl_assign_grades; no intervention log table; no mechanism linking AI-generated intervention to subsequent learning outcome change.

L5_Intervention_Delivery.discrepancy_note: DISCREPANCY: Consolidated average (50+25+0+0+25)/5 = 20%. Raw average = 30%. Paper reports 18%. None match exactly.

summary.resolution: The paper's Table 3 scores were produced in a separate scoring pass using the consolidated 5-dimension framework (COV/ACC/EDU/TMP/INT) but without a documented mapping to the original 32 raw sub-dimensions. The 'corrected_scores' above derive each Table 3 cell from the raw sub-dimensions using the 0/25/50/75/100 rubric scale with documented mappings. The paper must be updated to use either the corrected scores or re-justify the original Table 3 scores with per-cell evidence.
